CNN —

Baby, it’s over. The city of Paris started removing padlocks from the Pont des Arts on Monday, effectively ending the tourist tradition of attaching “love locks” to the bridge. For years, visitors have been attaching locks with sentimental messages to the bridge in symbolic acts of affection.  ...    It was considered charming at first, but the thrill wore off as sections of fencing on the Pont des Arts crumbled under the locks’ weight. title: Florence tries to stamp out locks of love | ITALY Magazine
highlights: decided to stamp out a tradition which has recently drawn thousands of young lovers to the city's famous Ponte Vecchio bridge. Enamoured couples have been going to the bridge to attach padlocks to a bronze bust and the railings around it. The act is seen as symbolising the 'unbreakable' bonds uniting them. title: Florence cuts through 'padlocks of love' - UPI.com
highlights: April 30 (UPI) -- Florence is trying to lock the door on the tradition of attaching a lock to the Italy's famous Ponte Vecchio, or "old bridge", as a symbol of undying love. For more than a decade, lovers who came to the oldest bridge in Florence have attached a padlock to the rails then thrown the key into the river. Grumpy council officials are trying to put a stop to the tradition to keep the unsightly clusters of metal from  ...    "As well as the aesthetic problem, these locks scratch and dent the metal," said cultural affairs chief Simone Siliani. A team of metal cutters spent the winter removing the 5,500 locks that had accumulated on the bridge. The work of removing the "padlocks of love" took so long because workmen had to battle against a never-ending flow of loving
